regulatory_framework.name: EU Delegated Regulation 2020/1818
key_requirements: CTB: minimum 30% GHG intensity reduction vs investment universe; 7% annual decarbonization trajectory from Base Date
PAB: minimum 50% GHG intensity reduction vs investment universe; 7% annual decarbonization trajectory from Base Date
Both CTB and PAB: exclude controversial weapons, red flag controversies, red/orange flag environmental controversies, tobacco manufacturing
PAB additional: exclude thermal coal mining (>=1% revenue), thermal coal distribution (any involvement), oil and gas activities (>=10% revenue), fossil fuel power generation (>=50% revenue)

document_metadata.title: MSCI EU CTB/PAB Overlay Indexes Methodology

applicable_universe.item: all existing constituents of Parent Index

universe.reference_index: Free float-adjusted market capitalization weighted index corresponding to the Parent Index per MSCI GIMI methodology; equals Parent Index when Parent follows GIMI
universe.eligibility_scope: Parent Index constituents filtered by Section 2.3 exclusion criteria

preprocessing.description: Security Level GHG Intensity (Scope 1+2+3) = (Unadjusted Scope 1+2 intensity + Unadjusted Scope 3 intensity) * (1 + EVIAF). Missing Scope 1+2 or EVIC: substitute average intensity of MSCI ACWI constituents in same GICS Industry Group. Missing Scope 3 or EVIC: substitute average Scope 3 intensity of MSCI ACWI constituents in same GICS Industry Group. EVIAF = (Average(EVIC) / Previous(Average(EVIC))) - 1.
preprocessing.source_section: Appendix I: Calculation of Target Metrics

content.model: MSCI Barra Global Equity Model for Long-Term Investors (GEMLTL)
content.objective: Minimize ex-ante tracking error relative to Parent Index subject to climate constraints (Table 2) and diversification constraints (Table 3)
content.source_section: 2.5 Optimization Constraints, 2.6 Determining the Optimized Index
content.no_rebalance_policy: If no feasible solution found after all constraint relaxations are exhausted, the Index is not rebalanced for that review

caps.note: Per-index overrides: +/-5% for MSCI Switzerland IMI EU PAB Overlay, MSCI France IMI EU PAB Overlay, MSCI France EU PAB Overlay (footnote 15)
Countries <2.5% of parent: upper bound = 3x parent country weight

schedules.name: semi-annual
schedules.data_cutoff: MSCI Solutions data as of end of month preceding the Index Review
schedules.announcement_lead: Pro forma Indexes in general announced nine business days before the effective date

base_date_reset.description: Base Date reset process defined in MSCI EU CTB/PAB Index Framework Sections 5.1 and 5.2. At the November 2025 Index Review, new Base Dates were calculated for each of the MSCI EU CTB/PAB Overlay Indexes that met the requirements of a Base Reset.
base_date_reset.source_section: Appendix II: Decarbonization Trajectory of Indexes

defers_to.section: Capped Weighted and Non-Market Capitalization Weighted indexes

intra_review_invariants.quote: No new securities will be added (except where noted below) to the Indexes between Index Reviews.
intra_review_invariants.concern: Spin-offs of existing constituents are the explicit exception: added immediately at event time, reevaluated at subsequent review.

intra_review_invariants.quote: A security will continue to be an Index constituent if there are changes in characteristics (country, sector, size segment, etc.)
intra_review_invariants.concern: Characteristics changes (country, sector, size segment) do not trigger deletion between reviews; reevaluation deferred to next review.

details.note: Companies not assessed on any of the three products are ineligible. For treatment of unrated companies in GHG intensity calculation (optimization target metrics), see Appendix I imputation rules.

new_rules.name: exclude_red_flag_controversies
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies with MSCI Controversy Score of 0 (Red Flag), indicating an ongoing Very Severe controversy implicating the company directly, to meet EU CTB/PAB controversy exclusion requirement.

source.markdown: Controversies: All companies assessed as having involvement in Controversies that are classified as Red Flags (MSCI Controversy Score of 0). A Red Flag indicates an ongoing, Very Severe controversy implicating a company directly through its actions, products, or operations.

business: assert (out['msci_controversy_score'] > 0).all(), f'Red Flag controversy score survived: min={out["msci_controversy_score"].min()}'
technical: assert 'msci_controversy_score' in out.columns, f'msci_controversy_score column missing: {out.columns}'

new_rules.name: exclude_red_orange_flag_environmental_controversies
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies with MSCI Environmental Controversy Score of 0 (Red Flag) or 1 (Orange Flag), covering ongoing severe environmental controversies, to meet EU CTB/PAB environmental harm exclusion requirement.

source.markdown: Environmental Harm: All companies assessed as having involvement in environmental controversies that are classified as Red (MSCI Environmental Controversy Score of 0) or Orange Flags (score of 1).

business: assert (out['msci_environmental_controversy_score'] > 1).all(), f'Red/Orange Flag environmental controversy score survived: min={out["msci_environmental_controversy_score"].min()}'
technical: assert 'msci_environmental_controversy_score' in out.columns, f'msci_environmental_controversy_score column missing: {out.columns}'

new_rules.name: exclude_tobacco
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies involved in manufacturing tobacco products to meet EU CTB/PAB tobacco exclusion requirement.

source.markdown: Tobacco: All companies that are involved in the manufacturing of Tobacco products.

technical: assert 'bisr_tobacco_manufacturing_flag' in out.columns, f'bisr_tobacco_manufacturing_flag column missing: {out.columns}'

new_rules.name: exclude_thermal_coal_mining
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies deriving 1% or more revenue from thermal coal mining sold to external parties, as required for PAB Overlay Indexes under EU 2020/1818.

source.markdown: Thermal Coal Mining: All companies deriving 1% or more revenue (either reported or estimated) from the mining of thermal coal (including lignite, bituminous, anthracite and steam coal) and its sale to external parties.

business: assert (out['bisr_thermal_coal_mining_revenue_pct'] < 1.0).all(), f'Thermal coal mining revenue >= 1% survived: max={out["bisr_thermal_coal_mining_revenue_pct"].max()}'
technical: assert 'bisr_thermal_coal_mining_revenue_pct' in out.columns, f'bisr_thermal_coal_mining_revenue_pct column missing: {out.columns}'

new_rules.name: exclude_thermal_coal_distribution
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies with evidence of thermal coal distribution or transport involvement, as required for PAB Overlay Indexes under EU 2020/1818.

source.markdown: Thermal Coal Distribution: All companies with evidence of thermal coal distribution or transport involvement. This includes transport of thermal coal by road, rail, shipping or air, and physical trading of thermal coal.

business: assert not out['bisr_thermal_coal_distribution_flag'].any(), f'Thermal coal distribution involvement survived: count={out["bisr_thermal_coal_distribution_flag"].sum()}'
technical: assert 'bisr_thermal_coal_distribution_flag' in out.columns, f'bisr_thermal_coal_distribution_flag column missing: {out.columns}'

new_rules.name: exclude_oil_gas_activities_pab
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies deriving 10% or more aggregate revenue from oil and gas related activities, as required for PAB Overlay Indexes under EU 2020/1818.

source.markdown: Oil & Gas Activities: All companies deriving 10% or more aggregate revenue from oil and gas related activities, including distribution / retail, equipment and services, extraction and production, pipelines and transportation and refining but excluding biofuel production and sales and trading activities.

technical: assert 'bisr_oil_gas_revenue_pct' in out.columns, f'bisr_oil_gas_revenue_pct column missing: {out.columns}'

new_rules.name: exclude_fossil_fuel_power_generation_pab
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies deriving 50% or more aggregate revenue from thermal coal, liquid fuel, or natural gas based power generation, as required for PAB Overlay Indexes under EU 2020/1818.

source.markdown: Power Generation: All companies deriving 50% or more aggregate revenue from thermal coal based power generation, liquid fuel based power generation and natural gas based power generation.

business: assert (out['bisr_fossil_fuel_power_generation_revenue_pct'] < 50.0).all(), f'Fossil fuel power generation revenue >= 50% survived: max={out["bisr_fossil_fuel_power_generation_revenue_pct"].max()}'

new_rules.name: exclude_unrated_msci_solutions
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies not assessed by MSCI Solutions on Controversies, Climate Change Metrics, or BISR, ensuring all constituents have complete ESG and climate data coverage.

source.markdown: Companies not assessed by MSCI Solutions on data for the following MSCI sustainability and climate products are not eligible for inclusion in the Indexes: MSCI Controversies, MSCI Climate Change Metrics, MSCI Business Involvement Screening Research (BISR)

business: assert out['is_rated_msci_controversies'].all(), f'Unrated Controversies company survived: count={int((~out["is_rated_msci_controversies"]).sum())}'
assert out['is_rated_msci_climate'].all(), f'Unrated Climate company survived: count={int((~out["is_rated_msci_climate"]).sum())}'
assert out['is_rated_msci_bisr'].all(), f'Unrated BISR company survived: count={int((~out["is_rated_msci_bisr"]).sum())}'
technical: assert 'is_rated_msci_controversies' in out.columns, f'is_rated_msci_controversies column missing: {out.columns}'
assert 'is_rated_msci_climate' in out.columns, f'is_rated_msci_climate column missing: {out.columns}'
assert 'is_rated_msci_bisr' in out.columns, f'is_rated_msci_bisr column missing: {out.columns}'

new_rules.name: optimize_index_weights
new_rules.purpose: Determine constituent weights via Barra Open Optimizer (GEMLTL model), minimizing ex-ante tracking error vs Parent Index subject to climate constraints (GHG intensity reduction vs Reference Index, 7% annual decarbonization trajectory, high-climate-impact sector active weight floor) and diversification constraints (constituent/sector/country active weight bounds, security weight multiple, turnover cap, factor and specific risk aversion). On infeasibility, relax one-way turnover in 1% steps up to 20% and active sector weight in 1% steps up to +/-20% alternately; if still infeasible, Index is not rebalanced.

source.markdown: At each Index Review, each Index is constructed using an optimization process that aims to achieve replicability and investability as well as minimize ex-ante tracking error relative to the Parent Index subject to the following constraints: 1. Climate objectives – constraints detailed in Table 2. 2. Diversification objectives – constraints detailed in Table 3.

business: assert abs(float(out['weight'].sum()) - 1.0) < 1e-6, f'Weights do not sum to 1: sum={out["weight"].sum()}'
assert (out['weight'] >= 0).all(), f'Negative weight in optimizer output: min={out["weight"].min()}'
technical: assert 'weight' in out.columns, f'weight column missing after optimization: {out.columns}'

new_rules.name: neutralize_intermediate_parent_reviews
new_rules.purpose: When the Parent Index undergoes more frequent reviews than the Index (e.g., quarterly vs semi-annual), neutralize changes made to the Parent Index during intermediate reviews to prevent unintended drift.

source.markdown: Additionally, if the frequency of Index Reviews in the Parent Index is greater than the frequency of Index Reviews in the Index, the changes made to the Parent Index during intermediate Index Reviews will be neutralized in the Index.

business: assert out.height > 0, f'No constituents after intermediate review neutralization: height={out.height}'
technical: assert 'weight' in out.columns, f'weight column missing after neutralization: {out.columns}'

new_rules.name: block_non_spinoff_parent_additions
new_rules.purpose: Prevent new Parent Index additions (IPOs and other early inclusions) from being added to the Index between Index Reviews. Spin-offs of existing Index constituents are the sole exception, added at event implementation and reevaluated at the subsequent review.

source.markdown: No new securities will be added (except where noted below) to the Indexes between Index Reviews.

business: assert out.height > 0, f'No constituents after blocking non-spinoff parent additions: height={out.height}'
technical: assert 'weight' in out.columns, f'weight column missing after addition-block: {out.columns}'

new_rules.name: reflect_parent_deletions
new_rules.purpose: Reflect Parent Index deletions in the Index simultaneously with the Parent Index so that securities removed from the Parent are also removed from the Index at the same time, maintaining alignment between the two.

source.markdown: Parent Index deletions will be reflected simultaneously.

business: assert out.height > 0, f'No constituents after reflecting parent deletions: height={out.height}'
technical: assert 'weight' in out.columns, f'weight column missing after parent-deletion reflection: {out.columns}'

reused_rules.name: exclude_controversial_weapons_involvement
reused_rules.justification: Section 2.3 criterion 1 applies the same controversial weapons exclusion as defined in the MSCI Ex-Controversial Weapons Indexes methodology, explicitly citing that methodology as the definition source.

mock_datapoints.name: msci_controversy_score
mock_datapoints.description: MSCI Controversies Score on a 0-10 scale; 0 = most severe (Red Flag).
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: A Red Flag indicates an ongoing, Very Severe controversy implicating a company directly through its actions, products, or operations (MSCI Controversy Score of 0).
definition_notes: MSCI Controversies Score falls on a 0-10 scale, with 0 being the most severe controversy.

mock_datapoints.name: msci_environmental_controversy_score
mock_datapoints.description: MSCI Environmental Controversy Score on a 0-10 scale; 0 = Red Flag, 1 = Orange Flag.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: A Red Flag indicates an ongoing, Very Severe controversy implicating a company directly through its actions, products, or operations. An Orange Flag indicates an ongoing Severe controversy implicating a company directly, or a Very Severe controversy that is either partially resolved or indirectly attributed to the company's actions, products, or operations.

mock_datapoints.name: bisr_controversial_weapons_flag
mock_datapoints.description: True if the company is involved in Controversial Weapons as defined by the MSCI Ex-Controversial Weapons Indexes methodology.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: All companies involved in Controversial Weapons as defined by the methodology of the MSCI Ex-Controversial Weapons Indexes.
definition_notes: Definition follows MSCI Ex-Controversial Weapons Indexes methodology.

mock_datapoints.name: bisr_tobacco_manufacturing_flag
mock_datapoints.description: True if the company is involved in the manufacturing of Tobacco products.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: All companies that are involved in the manufacturing of Tobacco products.

mock_datapoints.name: bisr_thermal_coal_mining_revenue_pct
mock_datapoints.description: Revenue percentage (reported or estimated) from mining of thermal coal sold to external parties.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: All companies deriving 1% or more revenue (either reported or estimated) from the mining of thermal coal (including lignite, bituminous, anthracite and steam coal) and its sale to external parties.
definition_notes: Covers lignite, bituminous, anthracite and steam coal.
Excludes revenue from metallurgical coal.
Excludes coal mined for internal power generation (e.g., vertically integrated power producers).
Excludes intra-company sales of mined thermal coal.
Excludes revenue from coal trading.

mock_datapoints.name: bisr_thermal_coal_distribution_flag
mock_datapoints.description: True if the company has evidence of thermal coal distribution or transport involvement.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: All companies with evidence of thermal coal distribution or transport involvement.
definition_notes: Includes transport of thermal coal by road, rail, shipping or air, and physical trading of thermal coal.
Does not include involvement in storage of thermal coal.
Does not include involvement in metallurgical coal-related activities.

mock_datapoints.name: bisr_oil_gas_revenue_pct
mock_datapoints.description: Aggregate revenue percentage from oil and gas related activities.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: All companies deriving 10% or more aggregate revenue from oil and gas related activities, including distribution / retail, equipment and services, extraction and production, pipelines and transportation and refining but excluding biofuel production and sales and trading activities.
definition_notes: Includes distribution/retail, equipment and services, extraction and production, pipelines and transportation, refining.
Excludes biofuel production and sales and trading activities.

mock_datapoints.name: bisr_fossil_fuel_power_generation_revenue_pct
mock_datapoints.description: Aggregate revenue percentage from thermal coal, liquid fuel, and natural gas based power generation.
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: All companies deriving 50% or more aggregate revenue from thermal coal based power generation, liquid fuel based power generation and natural gas based power generation.
definition_notes: Covers thermal coal based, liquid fuel based, and natural gas based power generation.
Per IPCC AR5 Chapter 7, these sources have median lifecycle emissions exceeding 100gCO2/kWh (source footnote 11).

mock_datapoints.name: ghg_intensity_scope123
mock_datapoints.description: Security Level GHG Intensity (Scope 1+2+3) in tCO2e per million USD EVIC.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix I: Calculation of Target Metrics
mock_datapoints.taxonomy: MSCI Climate Change Metrics GHG intensity
definition_notes: Formula: (Unadjusted Scope 1+2 intensity + Unadjusted Scope 3 intensity) * (1 + EVIAF).
Unadjusted intensity per scope = emissions / EVIC in M$.
Missing Scope 1+2 carbon emissions and/or EVIC: use average Scope 1+2 intensity of MSCI ACWI constituents in same GICS Industry Group.
Missing Scope 3 carbon emissions and/or EVIC: use average Scope 3 intensity of MSCI ACWI constituents in same GICS Industry Group.
EVIAF = (Average(EVIC) / Previous(Average(EVIC))) - 1.

mock_datapoints.name: parent_index_weight
mock_datapoints.description: Security weight in the Parent Index.

mock_datapoints.name: reference_index_weight
mock_datapoints.description: Security weight in the Reference Index (free float-adjusted market capitalization weighted per GIMI methodology).
definition_notes: For a Parent Index which follows the GIMI methodology, the Reference Index is the same as the Parent Index.

mock_datapoints.name: high_climate_impact_sector
mock_datapoints.description: True if the company is classified as belonging to a high climate impact sector.
mock_datapoints.taxonomy: MSCI EU CTB/PAB Index Framework climate impact sector classification
definition_notes: Classification defined in the MSCI EU CTB/PAB Index Framework (referenced by footnote 14 of Section 2.5).
Used in Table 2 constraint 3: minimum active weight in High Climate Impact Sector vs Reference Index = 0%.

mock_datapoints.name: is_rated_msci_controversies
mock_datapoints.description: True if the company has been assessed by MSCI Solutions for MSCI Controversies.

mock_datapoints.name: is_rated_msci_climate
mock_datapoints.description: True if the company has been assessed by MSCI Solutions for MSCI Climate Change Metrics.

mock_datapoints.name: is_rated_msci_bisr
mock_datapoints.description: True if the company has been assessed by MSCI Solutions for MSCI BISR.

final_asserts: assert abs(float(out['weight'].sum()) - 1.0) < 1e-6, f'Final weights do not sum to 1: sum={out["weight"].sum()}'
assert (out['weight'] >= 0).all(), f'Negative weight in final index: min={out["weight"].min()}'
assert (out['msci_controversy_score'] > 0).all(), f'Red Flag controversy constituent in final index: min score={out["msci_controversy_score"].min()}'
assert (out['msci_environmental_controversy_score'] > 1).all(), f'Red/Orange Flag environmental controversy constituent: min score={out["msci_environmental_controversy_score"].min()}'
assert not out['bisr_controversial_weapons_flag'].any(), f'Controversial weapons constituent in final index'
assert not out['bisr_tobacco_manufacturing_flag'].any(), f'Tobacco manufacturer in final index'
assert out['is_rated_msci_controversies'].all(), f'Unrated Controversies company in final index'
assert out['is_rated_msci_climate'].all(), f'Unrated Climate company in final index'
assert out['is_rated_msci_bisr'].all(), f'Unrated BISR company in final index'

external_references.section: Capped Weighted and Non-Market Capitalization Weighted indexes

scoped_out.quote: The MSCI EU CTB Overlay C-Series Indexes provide an alternative to the optimization-based approach used in Section 2.5 of this methodology book.
scoped_out.concern: Appendix V defines a separate non-optimized construction variant (C-Series) using iterative GHG intensity downweighting. Belongs in its own methbook variant with base_methbook='eu_ctb_pab_overlay', containing only the delta construction steps.

scoped_out.quote: The Methodology of constructing the MSCI Factor EU PAB Overlay differs from the above defined MSCI EU PAB Overlay Indexes due to the following constraints...
scoped_out.concern: Appendix VII defines the Factor EU PAB variant with different turnover caps (20% default, 25% Momentum) and relaxation schedules. Separate methbook variant with base_methbook='eu_ctb_pab_overlay'.

scoped_out.quote: The Methodology of constructing the MSCI Factor EU CTB Overlay differs from the above defined MSCI EU CTB Overlay Indexes due to the following constraints...
scoped_out.concern: Appendix VIII defines the Factor EU CTB variant with 20% turnover cap and specific relaxation schedule. Separate methbook variant with base_methbook='eu_ctb_pab_overlay'.

scoped_out.quote: In addition to the quarterly Index Reviews in February, May, August and November, the MSCI Momentum EU PAB Indexes undergo ad-hoc rebalancing subject to meeting certain trigger criteria.
scoped_out.concern: Appendix IX defers trigger logic entirely to MSCI Momentum Indexes Methodology Appendix III. No transformation defined in this document; belongs in the Momentum EU PAB variant methbook.

scoped_out.quote: A major new release of the relevant Barra Equity Model or Barra Optimizer may replace the former version within a suitable timeframe.
scoped_out.concern: Appendix IV is a policy statement about model version updates. No transformation defined.

Sections 4, 4.1, 4.1.1, 4.1.2, 4.2, 4.3 describe MSCI data products used. Product descriptions and methodology URLs only; no index construction transformations. Product dependencies captured in data_requirements.mandatory_products.

scoped_out.quote: The following sections have been modified as October 2022...
scoped_out.concern: Appendix XI is a historical changelog of methodology revisions. No transformations.
Document metadata and legal disclaimer. No transformations.

scoped_out.quote: The table below shows the Weighted Average GHG Intensity on the Base Date (W1) for each of the regions...
scoped_out.concern: Appendix II table of per-index W1 values and Base Dates is reference data consumed by the optimization constraint, not a transformation rule. Captured in optimize_index_weights thresholds (annual_decarbonization_rate=0.07) and review_cadence.base_date_reset.

clarifications_needed.quote: The MSCI EU CTB Overlay Indexes and the MSCI EU PAB Overlay Indexes
clarifications_needed.concern: The document covers CTB and PAB in a single methodology book, with PAB having four additional exclusion rules (thermal coal mining, thermal coal distribution, oil & gas activities, fossil fuel power generation) per Table 1. The apply() function must accept a CTB/PAB mode parameter to conditionally apply PAB-only rules, or PAB should be a separate variant methbook with base_methbook='eu_ctb_pab_overlay'. Current composition_order includes PAB-only rules; downstream implementation must handle the conditional.

clarifications_needed.quote: For the MSCI Switzerland IMI EU PAB Overlay Index, MSCI France IMI EU PAB Overlay Index and MSCI France EU PAB Overlay Index, the constituent active weight constraint relative to the Parent is '+/- 5%', instead of '+/- 2%'.
clarifications_needed.concern: The constituent active weight cap is index-specific: default +/-2%, but three named indexes use +/-5%. The optimize_index_weights rule captures the default threshold; per-index overrides require a kwarg or external config in apply() that this methbook does not define.

clarifications_needed.quote: For the MSCI France IMI EU CTB Overlay Index, the constituent active weight constraint relative to the Parent was relaxed to '-3%' instead of '-2%' at the May 2024 Index Review to achieve a feasible solution during the optimization process.
clarifications_needed.concern: A further one-off per-index active-weight asymmetric relaxation (-3%) applies to the MSCI France IMI EU CTB Overlay Index per footnote 16. Not expressible as a static threshold; requires per-index config.

clarifications_needed.quote: For Indexes where the Parent Index does not follow the MSCI GIMI methodology, the Parent Index methodology will define the GIMI Parent Index in the construction of this methodology book.
clarifications_needed.concern: Appendix VI states that for non-GIMI parent indexes, the parent's own methodology defines the Reference Index. This creates a per-index meta-dependency not capturable as a static rule; Reference Index identification requires external per-index configuration.

clarifications_needed.quote: exclude_red_flag_controversies, exclude_tobacco, exclude_thermal_coal_mining already exist in methbooks/rules/eligibility/
clarifications_needed.concern: These three rules exist in the rules directory but their originating methbook cannot be determined from the available information. They are listed here as new_rules; if they originate from another methbook, they should be moved to reused_rules with the correct methbook slug. The thresholds/definitions should be reconciled before duplicating.
